title: Surreal Photomontage / Vintage Collage
mood: dreamlike displacement, impossible geography, the everyday made mythic, beauty built from fragments

influences.name: Hannah Höch
influences.role: Dada photomontage — cutting up the world to reveal its absurdity
keys: figures constructed from mismatched sources as if they always belonged
bodies that are architecture, faces that are landscape
sharp scissor-edge cuts between incompatible realities
female figures reassembled by female logic, not male expectation
newspaper and magazine ephemera as raw material, never cleaned up

influences.name: Max Ernst
influences.role: surrealist collage novels, layered impossible dreamscapes
keys: Victorian engravings repurposed into nightmare and wonder
figures with animal or mechanical heads accepted without explanation
interiors that contain forests, forests that contain salons
scale as the primary surreal instrument — nothing the right size
narrative implied but never explained, the viewer must complete it

influences.name: Eugenia Loli
influences.role: modern digital retro collage — psychedelic vintage precision
keys: 1950s–70s source photographs: beach scenes, advertisements, scientific diagrams
natural forms (mushrooms, insects, clouds) scaled to impossible dominance
backgrounds that feel period-correct but contain anachronistic intrusions
colour grading that makes everything feel like a found slide
humour and menace coexisting in the same image

influences.name: Joe Webb
influences.role: minimal vintage photomontage — restraint as power
keys: black and white vintage photograph as anchor
one burst of saturated botanical or natural colour as the only relief
pure black or white backgrounds, nothing competing
flowers, plants, birds erupting from human heads or hands
the contrast between monochrome figure and living colour makes both more vivid

influences.name: 1960s Psychedelic Poster Art (Griffin, Moscoso, Wilson)
influences.role: colour intensity, optical density, surreal natural scale
keys: warm nocturnal palettes — teal sky, amber earth, deep green
light sources behaving like theatre: spot beams, aurora rays, halos
nature at heroic scale rendering humans into audience
starfields as backing texture, the cosmic as backdrop to the mundane
everything slightly too vivid to be real but too detailed to be fantasy

influences.name: Joseph Cornell
keys: each element feels selected, not generated — it was found, not invented
taxonomic materials: maps, specimen labels, atlas pages, reward posters
the image is also an archive of the image-maker's obsessions
ephemera as equal citizen to photograph
text fragments allowed to bleed into the visual as texture

bw_with_accent: any single saturated hue as contrast
forbidden: pure RGB primaries without vintage grade
antiseptic white backgrounds unless intentional contrast

construction_principles: SCALE VIOLATION is the primary surreal tool — make the small enormous, shrink the vast
CLEAN CUTS — edges are crisp, like physical scissors, no feathered blending between elements
LAYERED DEPTH — elements visibly sit on top of each other with intentional, owned overlap
SOURCE HETEROGENEITY — mix photograph, botanical illustration, engraving, newsprint, map, diagram in one image
HUMAN + NATURE FUSION — bodies host landscapes, heads explode into flora, figures become habitat
IMPOSSIBLE GEOGRAPHY — underwater mountains, beach with a cosmic ceiling, interiors opening to void
THEMATIC DENSITY — many symbols, each carrying weight, together building a surreal argument
FOUND MATERIAL FEEL — nothing should look generated from scratch; everything should feel sourced

figures: female portrait as axis — head, torso, or face as the canvas centre that other elements orbit
figures in period costume (Victorian, 1950s, Renaissance) placed in alien context
male figures often reduced to suits, uniforms, armour — identity via role not face
anatomy exposed: brains, ribcages, eyes multiplied, used decoratively not medically
nature: fungi — especially Amanita muscaria — at monumental scale
butterflies (especially Monarch) as symbol of transformation and fragility
crows and ravens perched with authority
botanical flowers (roses, poppies, anemones) in hyper-real clarity
insects: beetles, centipedes, moths used as jewellery or crown
ocean life placed in terrestrial or cosmic contexts
ephemera: vintage maps and atlas pages as backgrounds — topography as metaphor
reward posters, newspaper headlines, typeset labels bleeding into image
sheet music, book pages, handwritten text as texture layer
Victorian scientific diagrams — anatomical, botanical, astronomical
doilies, lace, gold decorative circles used as halos or frames
skies_and_space: starfields — deep space as background available to any scene
aurora or searchlight beams radiating from a central point
underwater caustic light patterns transplanted to terrestrial scenes
cloudscapes cut and repositioned inside interiors or over deserts

lighting: light as theatrical event — dramatic rays, god-beams, spotlight circles
nocturnal scenes lit from a single warm or cold source below or behind
vintage photography tone — slightly warm-shifted, as if aged or filtered
shadows are compositional shapes, not just absence of light
high-contrast B&W anchors with single saturated colour relief
underwater caustics used as ambient texture in non-underwater scenes
textures: vintage photo grain — the slight noise and halation of analog print
aged paper yellowing as underlying tone in lighter regions
engraving or woodcut linework on older illustrative elements
scan artifacts, newsprint dot patterns, ink bleed on text elements
no digital smoothness — every surface should feel like it has a material history
compositional_rules: central figure or face as gravitational anchor, other elements orbit or erupt from it
vertical element (giant mushroom, figure, beam of light) as primary axis
strong silhouette readability — the composition works as a shadow shape
foreground elements that break the frame or overlap the border
receding depth with multiple planes: close-up detail, mid-ground action, vast background
text and image cohabit — ephemera is not behind the image, it IS the image
symmetry broken deliberately — balance that slightly unnerves
crowded-yet-breathable: dense with elements but each has room to be seen

how_to_build_one.step_1_background: Start with an impossible environment: a vintage beach scene re-skied with deep space, an atlas page covering the whole canvas, an underwater panorama merged with mountain photography.
how_to_build_one.step_2_anchor: Place a central figure or dominant natural form (giant fungus, crowned creature, female portrait) that everything else will relate to.
how_to_build_one.step_3_scale_break: Introduce at least one element at a scale that violates physical reality. This is the core surreal move.
how_to_build_one.step_4_ephemera_layer: Add a layer of found-material texture: a map fragment, newsprint, botanical diagram, or reward poster. Blend mode Multiply or Overlay at low opacity.
how_to_build_one.step_5_nature_eruption: Route natural elements (flowers, butterflies, birds, fungi) into or out of the human figure — from the head, from the chest, from the hands.
how_to_build_one.step_6_colour_grade: Apply a unified vintage colour grade: warm shadows, slightly desaturated highlights, gentle yellow-brown tint over lights. OR strip to B&W and add one saturated element.
how_to_build_one.step_7_edges_and_cuts: Ensure all elements have clean, crisp edges — not feathered. The scissor-cut look is essential. No gradient fade-outs at element borders.
how_to_build_one.step_8_text_fragments: Optionally bleed text into the image as texture — titles, labels, numbers, partial words. Let them sit as equals with visual elements.

prompt_fragments.style_suffix: surreal photomontage collage, vintage found-material aesthetic, clean scissor-cut edges, layered paper ephemera, Hannah Höch dada photomontage, Max Ernst surrealist collage, 1960s psychedelic poster, scale violation, impossible geography
prompt_fragments.figure_suffix: female portrait as compositional axis, elements erupting from head, botanical flowers and ravens and exposed anatomy mixed with period clothing
prompt_fragments.environment_suffix: vintage retro background (1950s beach / atlas map / underwater panorama), starfield sky, god-beam light rays radiating from horizon, nocturnal warm palette
prompt_fragments.texture_suffix: analog film grain, aged paper yellowing, vintage photograph tones, newsprint dot texture, ephemera text fragments as visual texture
prompt_fragments.lighting_suffix: theatrical spotlight beams, nocturnal scene, single warm light source below horizon, deep shadow with silver rim-light on organic forms
prompt_fragments.negative_prompt: digital smooth gradients, feathered edges, neon glow, pure CGI render, antiseptic clean, modern minimalism, flat illustration, anime, 3D render artifacts, photorealistic studio lighting

inpaint_prompt_templates.add_nature_to_figure: botanical flowers erupting from [body part], lush roses and poppies cascading, vintage collage style, clean cut edges, surreal photomontage
inpaint_prompt_templates.scale_violation: giant [subject] looming over tiny [scene], scale impossible, 1960s vintage aesthetic, nocturnal warm palette, surreal photomontage collage
inpaint_prompt_templates.ephemera_background: vintage atlas map page, topographic lines, aged yellowed paper, faint text and numbers, collage texture layer
inpaint_prompt_templates.sky_replacement: deep space starfield, aurora searchlight rays radiating from horizon, nocturnal teal and gold, vintage photograph grain
inpaint_prompt_templates.creature_with_crown: surreal crowned creature assembled from [animal parts], jeweled regalia, monarch butterfly wings, impossible regal bearing, photomontage collage

comfyui_workflow_notes.best_model: realistic-vision or dreamshaper for photomontage realism; stable-diffusion-xl for richer detail
notes: Use img2img inpaint for adding nature-eruption effects to existing figure layers
ControlNet Canny works well for preserving clean cut-out edge quality
ControlNet Depth helps maintain the layered-depth feel when adding background elements
Keep CFG 7–9 for photomontage blend — lower than pure generation to preserve source character
